note: Server remains current_thread flavor. Per-field locking reduces lock granularity but does NOT enable true parallelism. Multi-client workloads still serialize on one OS thread.

4c_async_medium.verdict: No improvement over Phase 12 (expected: current_thread runtime prevents parallelism)

8c_async_medium.verdict: Slight improvement from more client-side parallelism in push encoding, not server-side

4c_batch_medium.verdict: Massive improvement from batch framing — clients overlap encoding with server processing

1c_batch_medium.verdict: 2.7x improvement over Phase 13 baseline — per-field locking reduces contention with background tasks

4c_async_small.verdict: Consistent with medium — pipeline size does not affect multi-client async throughput

4c_async_large.verdict: No HLL regression under multi-client load — consistent with small/medium

gates.4c_exceeds_28k: MARGINAL — 27.7k vs 28k baseline, essentially flat (within measurement noise)
gates.1c_within_10pct_regression: PASS — 135.6k eps is -4.5% vs 142k (200k event methodology)

analysis.why_no_multi_client_improvement: Server uses tokio current_thread (src/main.rs line 26). All TCP connections are multiplexed on a single OS thread. Per-field locking and DashMap only reduce lock contention granularity — they cannot enable parallelism when there is only one thread. The PLMutex<StateStore> deviation from Plan 14-01 further limits benefit since store access is still serialized behind a single mutex.
analysis.why_batch_improved: push_many batches amortize Python SDK overhead (encoding, syscalls) per 1000 events. The server processes each batch as a single lock acquisition. Multi-client batch workloads overlap client-side batch encoding time with server-side processing of other clients' batches, even on a single thread — this is async I/O pipelining, not true parallelism.
analysis.path_to_real_concurrency: Switch main.rs to #[tokio::main] (multi-thread flavor). This alone would let the per-field locks deliver their intended benefit: different connections handling events for different streams truly in parallel on separate OS threads.
